costs: Design and validation costs for new exam item banks and SES-index construction
Administrative burden of outreach programs at disadvantaged feeder schools
Risk of formula complexity reducing public trust in exam fairness
content.equity_impact: Likely modest positive equity effect at the gimnázium entry gate itself, but does not address upstream primary-school sorting, church-school admissions discretion, or Roma segregation, which operate through separate mechanisms (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07, F-live-polish_reform-08).
expected_benefits: Reduced weight of purchasable exam-prep advantage in determining admission outcomes
Potential narrowing of the tertiary-educated-parent overrepresentation documented in 6/8-year cohorts
Preserves politically entrenched track structure, lowering legal and political barriers to enactment
content.goal: Reduce family-capital-driven sorting at the point of gimnázium entry while preserving the current 4/6/8-year track structure.

implementation_steps.action: Commission redesign of math/Hungarian exam items toward reasoning-based, less-coachable formats and pilot SES-linked scoring adjustments (e.g., feeder-school disadvantage index) in a subset of districts
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Education / centralized exam authority

implementation_steps.action: Mandate outreach programs at disadvantaged-population feeder primary schools to raise application rates prior to exam rollout
Evaluate pilot results against baseline admission composition data and adjust SES-weighting formula
Enact any statutory quota/proxy provisions found necessary beyond ministerial discretion
implementation_steps.actor: Parliament (if quota component requires legal basis)

mechanisms: Uniform national exam already exists and is centrally graded, giving an existing lever to redesign item types toward coaching-resistance without new legal architecture (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02).

Documented coaching investment (3.5 hrs/week, 34,000 HUF/month) concentrated among university-educated parents shows the exam interacts with a private prep market that a redesigned exam format could weaken (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05).
Interview weight is already capped at 25% by regulation, providing a lever to reduce or reallocate subjective components that may favor coached/higher-SES applicants (F-live-legal_and_governance-03).

Distance to nearest 6-/8-year school is non-random and clusters in urban areas, meaning SES-aware entry adjustments would need to also account for geographic access gaps to be effective (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08).

Elementary schools serving disadvantaged populations often do not facilitate participation in the entry competition at all, indicating an admissions-mechanism fix alone may not reach non-applicant pools (F-live-education_finance-02).
risks: SES-index proxies may be gamed or contested as unfair by advantaged families with political access (F-live-political_feasibility-07)
Reform does not touch free school choice or primary-school sorting occurring before age 10, which independently drives segregation (F-live-portuguese_reform-06, F-live-polish_reform-07)
Coaching-resistant redesign may simply shift coaching investment to new exam formats rather than eliminating the advantage
content.title: Coaching-resistant, SES-weighted admission redesign for existing 6/8-year tracks
